title: hieracium onosmoides subsp. crinulifolium
intro: Hieracium onosmoides subsp. crinulifolium, a lesser-known subspecies of the Hieracium genus, is a flowering plant characterized by its fern-like foliage and clusters of small dandelion-like flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium onosmoides subsp. crinulifolium are said to include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as an infusion or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
